<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="cnt">Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <equals>
                        <input_ref id="rst_n"/>
                        <bit>0</bit>
                    </equals>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <output_ref id="cnt"/> = 4'b0000;
                    </assign>
                    <assign>
                        <output_ref id="dout_parallel"/> = 8'b00000000;
                    </assign>
                    <assign>
                        <output_ref id="dout_valid"/> = 1'b0;
                    </assign>
                </then>
                <else>
                    <if>
                        <condition>
                            <equals>
                                <input_ref id="din_valid"/>
                                <bit>1</bit>
                            </equals>
                        </condition>
                        <then>
                            <assign>
                                <output_ref id="cnt"/> = <output_ref id="cnt"/> + 4'b0001;
                            </assign>
                            <if>
                                <condition>
                                    <equals>
                                        <output_ref id="cnt"/> 
                                        <binary>1000</binary>
                                    </equals>
                                </condition>
                                <then>
                                    <assign>
                                        <output_ref id="dout_parallel"/> = <shift><input_ref id="dout_parallel"/> <input_ref id="din_serial"/></shift>;
                                    </assign>
                                    <assign>
                                        <output_ref id="dout_valid"/> = 1'b1;
                                    </assign>
                                    <assign>
                                        <output_ref id="cnt"/> = 4'b0000;
                                    </assign>
                                </then>
                                <else>
                                    <assign>
                                        <output_ref id="dout_valid"/> = 1'b0;
                                    </assign>
                                </else>
                            </if>
                        </then>
                    </if>
                </else>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Design a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit numbers. Clock signal. Reset signal. Signed or unsigned operation. Input operand DIVIDEND. Input operand DIVISOR. Valid operation request. The output has been acknowledged. The result is valid. Output result. 0 1 2 3 4 5 Counter for the division process. Start control for the division process. Two's complement of the divisor. Shift register for the division process.

When

opn_valid is high and res_valid is low,

the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor.

Initializes the shift register sr with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.

Initializes neg_divisor to the negated absolute value of the divisor.

Initializes the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.

When start_cnt is high,

  1. If the counter cnt reaches 8 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete:

    • cnt and start_cnt are cleared.
    • Updates the shift register sr with the final remainder and quotient.

  2. Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented,

    and the shift register sr is updated based on the subtraction result:

    • sub_result is computed as the subtraction of neg_divisor.
    • mux_result is selected based on the carry-out.
    • sr is shifted left and updated with the carry-out.

When res_valid is high and res_ready is low,

res_valid is cleared to low.

Otherwise, if res_valid is high and res_ready is high,

res_valid is set to low.

<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="flag">Flag signal for internal state.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="data_lock" width_description="[7:0]">Register for temporarily storing the first valid input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="valid_out_reg">Register for storing the valid output signal.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="flag_reg">Register for storing the flag signal.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <switch>
            <case condition="'flag_reg' == '0'">
                <text>data_lock = data_in;</text>
            </case>
            <case condition="'flag_reg' == '1'">
                <text>data_out = {data_lock, data_in};</text>
            </case>
        </switch>
        <switch>
            <case condition="'flag_reg' == '0'">
                <text>valid_out_reg = 0;</text>
            </case>
            <case condition="'flag_reg' == '1'">
                <text>valid_out_reg = 1;</text>
            </case>
        </switch>
        <switch>
            <case condition="'flag_reg' == '0'">
                <text>flag_reg = 1;</text>
            </case>
            <case condition="'flag_reg' == '1'">
                <text>flag_reg = 0;</text>
            </case>
        </switch>
    </implementation>
</module>

module pe(
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    output reg [31:0] c
);
always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
  if (rst) begin
    c <= 0;
  end else begin
    c <= c + a * b;
  end
end
endmodule

<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5-clock cycle periods of yellow and 10-clock cycle periods of red. The north-south (NS) light should have a 60-clock cycle period, with 5-clock cycle periods of yellow and 10-clock cycle periods of red. The period of the cycle should be the same for both the EW and NS light, and the period of the cycle is not affected by the pedestrian button. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time for the EW light is greater than 10 clocks, then the green time is shortened to 10 clocks. If the remaining green time for the EW light is less than 10 clocks, then the green time remains unchanged. The east-west and north-south lights are paired, so that when the EW light is green, the NS light is red; when the EW light is yellow, the NS light is red; and when the EW light is red, the NS light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">State.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[7:0]">Cnt.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">P_state.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">P_red.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">P_yellow.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[0:0]">P_green.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 green.</enum>
        <case>
            <case_item id="idle">
                <if condition="state==s1_red">state = s1_red;</if>
            </case_item>
            <case_item id="s1_red">
                <if condition="cnt==3">state = s3_green;</if>
                <else>state = s1_red;</else>
            </case_item>
            <case_item id="s2_yellow">
                <if condition="cnt==3">state = s1_red;</if>
                <else>state = s2_yellow;</else>
            </case_item>
            <case_item id="s3_green">
                <if condition="cnt==3">state = s2_yellow;</if>
                <else>state = s3_green;</else>
            </case_item>
        </case>
        <always_combinational_logic>
            <if condition="!rst_n">cnt = 10;</if>
            <else>
                <if condition="pass_request && green">cnt = 10;</if>
                <else>
                    <if condition="!green && p_green">cnt = 60;</if>
                    <else>
                        <if condition="!yellow && p_yellow">cnt = 5;</if>
                        <else>
                            <if condition="!red && p_red">cnt = 10;</if>
                        </else>
                    </else>
                </else>
            </else>
        </always_combinational_logic>
        <assign width_description="[7:0]" id="clock">cnt;</assign>
        <always_clocked_sequential_logic>
            <if condition="!rst_n">
                <assign id="p_red">0;</assign>
                <assign id="p_yellow">0;</assign>
                <assign id="p_green">0;</assign>
            </assign>
            <else>
                <assign id="p_red">red;</assign>
                <assign id="p_yellow">yellow;</assign>
                <assign id="p_green">green;</assign>
            </else>
        </always_clocked_sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. Parameter describing the bit-width of the multiplicand and multiplier, with a value of 8. Register to store the input enable signal, mul_en_in, and control the multiplication operation. Register to store the input multiplicand, mul_a. Register to store the input multiplier, mul_b. Register to accumulate the partial sums. Register to store the final product value. Wire to store the partial products. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input enable signal and stores it in a register, mul_en_out_reg. This register holds the input enable signal value for the next clock cycle. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input values and stores them in the corresponding registers, mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg. These registers hold the input multiplicand and multiplier values for the multiplication operation. The module generates partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. It uses conditional assignments to select the appropriate bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits. The partial products are stored in a set of wires, temp, with each wire representing a different bit position. The module performs addition operations on the partial products to calculate partial sums. It uses a set of registers, sum, to store the intermediate sum values. Each register corresponds to a group of partial products and is updated on the positive edge of the clock signal. The module sums up all the partial sums to obtain the final product. It uses a register, mul_out_reg, to store the accumulated sum. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the register is updated with the sum of all partial sums. The module assigns the output product value, mul_out, based on the output enable signal and the value in mul_out_reg. If the output enable signal is active, indicating a valid product, the value in mul_out_reg is assigned to mul_out. Otherwise, mul_out is set to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Width of parallel data input. Count of cycles for conversion.

Initialize the counter (cnt) to 0 and valid signal (valid) to 0.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is 1, increment the counter (cnt) by 1.

When the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, update the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), reset the counter (cnt) to 0, and set the valid signal (valid) to 1.

Otherwise, increment the counter (cnt) by 1, set the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shift the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.

    <description>Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.</description>

        <output id="odd" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit remainder.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">First 8-bit input operand (representing a multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Second 8-bit input operand (representing a multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the product of the two 8-bit inputs (A * B).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="n">8</parameter>
        <parameter id="m">16</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="shift_A" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand after shifting.</reg>
            <reg id="product_temp" width_description="[15:0]">Product after each iteration.</reg>
            <reg id="counter" width_description="[3:0]">Counter for the number of iterations.</reg>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic>
            <assign id="A" value="shift_A"/> 
            <assign id="product" value="product_temp"/> 
            <always>
                <if>
                    <condition>
                        <and>
                            <id>counter</id>
                            <value>0</value>
                        </and>
                    </condition>
                    <then>
                        <assign id="shift_A" value="A"/> 
                        <assign id="product_temp" value="0"/> 
                        <assign id="counter" value="0"/> 
                    </then>
                </if>
                <else/>
                <for>
                    <from>
                        <id>counter</id>
                    </from>
                    <to>
                        <id>n-1</id>
                    </to>
                    <step>
                        <id>counter</id>
                        <value>1</value>

                    <body>

                                <xor>
                                    <id>B[0]</id>

                            </condition>

                                </xor>
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <assign id="product_temp" value="product_temp + shift_A"/>

                        </if>
                        <if>

                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <assign id="product_temp" value="product_temp + shift_A"/>

                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <assign id="product_temp" value="product_temp + shift_A"/>

                                </xor>
                            </condition>

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Detect a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 10.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 100.</enum>
        <enum id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1001.</enum>
        <enum id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 10011.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="MATCH" type="sequential_logic">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The FSM detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, the output MATCH is 1. The FSM supports continuous input and loop detection. The state transitions are based on the input IN. The next state is determined based on the current state and the value of IN. The output MATCH is set to 1 when the input IN is 10011. The FSM has five states: S0, S1, S2, S3, and S5. The FSM is reset to S0 when RST is active. The FSM transitions between states based on the value of IN. When the FSM is in state S5, the output MATCH is set to 1 if IN is 1, and the next state is S1 if IN is 1. If IN is not 1, the output MATCH is set to 0, and the next state is the current state.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Design a frequency divider that divides the input clock frequency of 100MHz into 50MHz, 10MHz, and 1MHz.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="CLK_in">Input clock signal</parameter>
        <parameter id="RST">Reset signal</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="counter_50">
            <description>Reset the counter on the positive edge of RST or the negative edge of CLK_in. If RST is active, set CLK_50 to 0. Otherwise, toggle CLK_50.</description>
            <condition>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_in" operator="!" /><operator id="CLK_50" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </condition>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set CLK_50 to 1 if RST is active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_50" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set CLK_50 to the negation of CLK_50 if RST is not active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="CLK_50" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="counter_10">
            <description>Reset the counter on the positive edge of RST or the negative edge of CLK_in. If RST is active, set CLK_10 to 0 and the counter cnt_10 to 0. Otherwise, increment the counter cnt_10.</description>
            <condition>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_in" operator="!" /><operator id="CLK_10" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </condition>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set CLK_10 to 1 if RST is active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_10" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set the counter cnt_10 to 0 if RST is active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="cnt_10" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set the counter cnt_10 to the negation of cnt_10 if RST is not active and CLK_10 is not active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="cnt_10" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="counter_1">
            <description>Reset the counter on the positive edge of RST or the negative edge of CLK_in. If RST is active, set CLK_1 to 0 and the counter cnt_100 to 0. Otherwise, increment the counter cnt_100.</description>
            <condition>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_in" operator="!" /><operator id="CLK_1" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </condition>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set CLK_1 to 1 if RST is active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_1" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set the counter cnt_100 to 0 if RST is active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="cnt_100" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
            <assignment>
                <description>Set the counter cnt_100 to the negation of cnt_100 if RST is not active and CLK_1 is not active.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="cnt_100" operator="!" />
                </expression>
            </assignment>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <description>Implement the frequency divider using counters. Refer to the implementation details in the description.</description>
        <implementation>
            <sequential>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_in" operator="!" /><operator id="CLK_50" operator="!" />
                </expression>
                <description>Reset the counter on the positive edge of RST or the negative edge of CLK_in. If RST is active, set CLK_50 to 0. Otherwise, toggle CLK_50.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_50" operator="!" />
                </expression>
                <description>Reset the counter on the positive edge of RST or the negative edge of CLK_in. If RST is active, set CLK_10 to 0 and the counter cnt_10 to 0. Otherwise, increment the counter cnt_10.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_in" operator="!" /><operator id="CLK_10" operator="!" />
                </expression>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="cnt_10" operator="!" />
                </expression>
                <description>Reset the counter on the positive edge of RST or the negative edge of CLK_in. If RST is active, set CLK_1 to 0 and the counter cnt_100 to 0. Otherwise, increment the counter cnt_100.</description>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="CLK_in" operator="!" /><operator id="CLK_1" operator="!" />
                </expression>
                <expression>
                    <operator id="RST" /><operator id="cnt_100" operator="!" />

<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">ALU control signal.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="ADD" value="6'b100000">ADD</enum>
        <enum id="ADDU" value="6'b100001">ADDU</enum>
        <enum id="SUB" value="6'b100010">SUB</enum>
        <enum id="SUBU" value="6'b100011">SUBU</enum>
        <enum id="AND" value="6'b100100">AND</enum>
        <enum id="OR" value="6'b100101">OR</enum>
        <enum id="XOR" value="6'b100110">XOR</enum>
        <enum id="NOR" value="6'b100111">NOR</enum>
        <enum id="SLT" value="6'b101010">SLT</enum>
        <enum id="SLTU" value="6'b101011">SLTU</enum>
        <enum id="SLL" value="6'b000000">SLL</enum>
        <enum id="SRL" value="6'b000010">SRL</enum>
        <enum id="SRA" value="6'b000011">SRA</enum>
        <enum id="SLLV" value="6'b000100">SLLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRLV" value="6'b000110">SRLV</enum>
        <enum id="SRAV" value="6'b000111">SRAV</enum>
        <enum id="LUI" value="6'b001111">LUI</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
For example, if size = 4 and mul_a = 2, then the extended input will be 00000010.
The module uses a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.
For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
The partial products are added to obtain the final product.
The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.

<module id="calendar">
    <description>Implement a perpetual calendar.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal.</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">Current hours.</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">Current minutes.</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">Current seconds.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="Secs">Seconds counter.</parameter>
        <parameter id="Mins">Minutes counter.</parameter>
        <parameter id="Hours">Hours counter.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block for updating the seconds value based on the clock and reset signals.
        If the reset signal is active, set the seconds value to 0.
        If the seconds value is 59, wrap around and set the seconds value to 0.
        Otherwise, increment the seconds value by 1.
        Always block for updating the minutes value based on the clock and reset signals.
        If the reset signal is active, set the minutes value to 0.
        If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, wrap around and set the minutes value to 0.
        If the seconds value is 59, increment the minutes value by 1.
        Otherwise, keep the minutes value unchanged.
        Always block for updating the hours value based on the clock and reset signals.
        If the reset signal is active, set the hours value to 0.
        If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum, wrap around and set the hours value to 0.
        If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, increment the hours value by 1.
        Otherwise, keep the hours value unchanged.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="counter_12">
    <description>Counter from 0 to 11, controlled by valid_count.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low)</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal</input>
        <input id="valid_count">Enable counting</input>
        <output id="out" width_description="[3:0]">Current count</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="STATE_IDLE" value="0x0">Idle state</enum>
        <enum id="STATE_COUNT" value="0x1">Counting state</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <if>
                <condition>
                    <and>
                        <item>rst_n == 0</item>
                    </and>
                </condition>
                <then>
                    <assign>
                        <object>@out</object>
                        <value>4'b0000</value>
                    </assign>
                </then>
            </if>
            <else>
                <if>
                    <condition>
                        <and>
                            <item>valid_count == 1</item>
                            <item>out == 4'd11</item>
                        </and>
                    </condition>
                    <then>
                        <assign>
                            <object>@out</object>
                            <value>4'b0000</value>
                        </assign>
                    </then>
                </if>
                <else>
                    <if>
                        <condition>
                            <and>
                                <item>valid_count == 1</item>
                            </and>
                        </condition>
                        <then>
                            <assign>
                                <object>@out</object>
                                <value>out + 4'b0001</value>
                            </assign>
                        </then>
                    </if>
                </else>
            </else>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

The method of empty and full judgment is to generate empty and full signals by comparing the Gray code. Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth 8. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower three digits of the binary number as the address to access RAM. When the read and write Pointers are equal, the FIFO is null.

When the write pointer has one more cycle RAM than the read pointer, the highest and second-highest bits of the read and write pointer are opposite, the remaining bits are the same, and the FIFO is full. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Use dual-port RAM module to store data.

Use 4-bit Gray code as a read/write pointer for a FIFO with depth DEPTH. The gray code is converted to a four-digit binary number, using the lower $clog2(DEPTH) bits of the binary number as the address to access RAM.

Use the following code to convert Gray code to binary:

binary = (gray & (1 << $clog2(DEPTH))) | (binary & ~(1 << $clog2(DEPTH))) | (binary << 1);

Use the following code to convert binary to Gray code:

gray = (binary & (1 << $clog2(DEPTH))) | ((binary & ~ (1 << $clog2(DEPTH))) & (1 << $clog2(DEPTH)));

Use the following code to perform two-stage trigger:

reg [1:0] buff;
always @(posedge clk or negedge rstn) begin
        if (~rstn) begin
            buff <= 2'b00;
        end else begin
            buff <= {buff[0], signal};
        end
end

<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave signal generator.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Output waveform.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="state">State register used for controlling the waveform generation.</param>
        <param id="wave">Waveform register representing the generated signal.</param>
    </param_description>
    <implementation>
    The module generates a triangle waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave". The waveform cycles between 0 and 31 by using a state machine. The state machine controls the waveform generation based on the current state of the signal.
    The module includes a 5-bit register named "wave" for the generated waveform and a 1-bit register named "state" for the state of the waveform generation.
    The state and waveform registers are updated in an always block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
    On reset (~rst_n), the state register is cleared to 0, and the wave register is also cleared to 0.
    The waveform generation is controlled by a case statement based on the current state:
    - If the state is 0, the waveform is incremented by 1.
    - If the waveform reaches 31 (wave == 31), the state is transitioned to 1.
    - If the state is 1, the waveform is decremented by 1.
    - If the waveform reaches 0 (wave == 0), the state is transitioned back to 0.
    The waveform generation continues to cycle between 0 and 31 based on the state transitions.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a module of a 64-bit ripple carry adder, which includes several registers to enable the pipeline stages.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="PIPE_STAGES" value_description="[2:0]">Pipeline stages of the adder.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>Registers to enable the pipeline stages.</description>
        <reg id="i_en_reg" width_description="[2:0]">Pipeline stage enable signals.</reg>
        <reg id="adda_reg" width_description="[63:0]">Input operand A for each pipeline stage.</reg>

module pulse_detect(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output data_out
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description: Implement a module for pulse detection. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). One-bit input signal. Output signal indicating the presence of pulses. Initial state of the pulse detection process. State indicating that a pulse is detected. State indicating that a pulse is detected. State indicating the end of a pulse. Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process. Initialize state to S0. state = S0; State transition logic. always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (~rst_n) begin state <= S0; end else begin case (state) S0: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S1; end else begin state <= S0; end end S1: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S2; end else begin state <= S0; end end S2: begin if (~data_in) begin state <= S3; end else begin state <= S2; end end S3: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S1; end else begin state <= S0; end end default: state <= S0; endcase end end Declare a register data_out to keep track of the output signal. Initialize data_out to 0. data_out = 1'b0; Output logic. always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (~rst_n) begin data_out <= 1'b0; end else begin case (state) S0: data_out <= 1'b0; S1: data_out <= 1'b0; S2: data_out <= 1'b0; S3: data_out <= 1'b1; default: data_out <= 1'b0; endcase end end Implement the pulse detection process. always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (~rst_n) begin state <= S0; data_out <= 1'b0; end else begin case (state) S0: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S1; data_out <= 1'b0; end else begin state <= S0; data_out <= 1'b0; end end S1: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S2; data_out <= 1'b0; end else begin state <= S0; data_out <= 1'b0; end end S2: begin if (~data_in) begin state <= S3; data_out <= 1'b0; end else begin state <= S2; data_out <= 1'b0; end end S3: begin if (data_in) begin state <= S1; data_out <= 1'b1; end else begin state <= S0; data_out <= 1'b0; end end default: begin state <= S0; data_out <= 1'b0; end endcase end end

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate input data and output after 4 inputs.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Valid input signal.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid output signal.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter name="READY">1</parameter>
        <parameter name="PROCESS">2</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="current_state" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Next state.</reg>
        <reg id="cnt" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[1:0]">Counter.</reg>
        <reg id="sum" type="combinational_logic" width_description="[9:0]">Accumulated sum.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module operates in a state machine fashion. The states include IDLE, READY, and PROCESS. The module transitions from IDLE to READY when valid_in is asserted. In the READY state, the module waits for valid_in to be asserted for another cycle. In the PROCESS state, the module accumulates data_in values for 4 cycles and outputs the accumulated sum. The valid_out signal is asserted for one cycle when the data_out is updated. Before the first data_in input, the sum is 0. During the PROCESS state, the module accumulates the data_in values and updates the sum. The data_out is updated with the sum value when in the PROCESS state. The valid_out signal is asserted for one cycle when the data_out is updated. The module resets to IDLE state when rst_n is asserted.
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. Width of the data. Depth of the RAM.

Define a 8*6 RAM array.

Trigger always block on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).

On reset, indicated by !rst_n, the read_data register is cleared to 0.

Read the data at the specified address (read_addr) in the RAM array and assign it to the read_data register if the read_en signal is active.

Width of the input and output signals. sequential_logic Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Input signal to be right-shifted. Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.

Initialize the q register to 0 using the initial statement.

On each rising edge of the clock, right-shift the contents of the q register by 1 bit using the >> operator.

Insert the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.

Assign the value of the input signal to the most significant bit (MSB) of the register using the assignment statement.

samples.xml_description:
Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer, data_in will remain constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. Clock signal A. Clock signal B. Active-low reset signal A. Active-low reset signal B. Input data signal. Input enable signal. Output data signal. Idle state. Wait for enable signal. Synchronize data. The module includes a register, data_reg, to store the input data signal, data_in.
On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the register (data_reg) is set to 0.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (data_reg) is updated with the input data signal (data_in).
The module includes a register, en_data_reg, to store the input enable signal, data_en.
On the positive edge of clock signal A (clk_a) or the falling edge of reset signal A (arstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is low, the register (en_data_reg) is set to 0.
If the reset signal A (arstn) is high, the register (en_data_reg) is updated with the input enable signal (data_en).
The module includes two registers, en_clap_one and en_clap_two, to control the selection of the input data.
On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the registers.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, both registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are set to 0.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is high, the registers (en_clap_one and en_clap_two) are updated based on the value of en_data_reg.
The register en_clap_one is assigned the value of en_data_reg, and en_clap_two is assigned the previous value of en_clap_one.
The module includes a register, out_data_reg, to store the output data signal, dataout.
On the positive edge of clock signal B (clk_b) or the falling edge of reset signal B (brstn), the module updates the register.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is low, indicating a reset condition, the output data (dataout) is set to 0.
If the reset signal B (brstn) is high and the control signal (en_clap_two) is active, the output data (dataout) is assigned the value of the data register (data_reg).
If the control signal (en_clap_two) is inactive, the output data (dataout) retains its previous value.
